protocol.name: playos_compositor_control_v1
protocol.copyright:
Copyright (c) 2026 PlayOS Foundation
Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions:
The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all copies or substantial portions of the Software.
protocol.description:
Control interface for runtime services (playos-sessiond) to manage the compositor's presentation state. The runtime binds as a Wayland client and uses this protocol to request shell/game activation and receive lifecycle events.
This is the Stage 2 IPC channel between the compositor and the runtime. Future stages will add overlay control, display-mode management, and presentation-state queries.

interface.name: playos_compositor_control_v1
interface.description:
A global interface for runtime-to-compositor control. Only one client may bind this interface at a time (the runtime). If a second client binds, the compositor will close the connection.
Requests flow from the runtime to the compositor; events flow from the compositor to the runtime.

request.name: activate_shell

description.summary: close the active game and show the shell

request.description:
Close the currently active game toplevel (if any) and bring the shell to the foreground. If no game is active, this is a no-op — the shell (if alive) remains in the foreground.
This is equivalent to pressing the Home button.

event.name: game_closed
event.description: Sent when the active game toplevel is destroyed, whether by Home-button press, client disconnect, crash, or an explicit activate_shell request. The runtime should use this event to clean up process state and prepare for the next game launch.

event.name: shell_closed
event.description: Sent when the shell toplevel is destroyed. The runtime should restart the shell process when it receives this event.
